task_completion.description: Open the 'Create a new project' dialog in Visual Studio.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Create a new project' window is visible in the Visual Studio interface.

task_completion.description: Select the C# Console App project template.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Console App' template with the 'C#' tag is highlighted or selected in the list of project templates.
The 'Project name' text field in the configuration dialog contains exactly 'HelloWorld'.

task_completion.description: Set the project location to ensure the final path is C:\Users\Docker\source\repos\HelloWorld.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Location' text field shows 'C:\Users\Docker\source\repos' (with project name HelloWorld) or 'C:\Users\Docker\source\repos\HelloWorld'.

task_completion.description: Verify the new project successfully loads in the Visual Studio editor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main Visual Studio IDE window is visible with the 'HelloWorld' project displayed in the Solution Explorer panel, typically with 'Program.cs' visible or open.

integrity.description: The agent used the Visual Studio GUI to create the project, not terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with Visual Studio dialog boxes. There should be no command prompt or PowerShell windows executing 'dotnet new' commands.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with Visual Studio 2022, not another editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI must match the full Visual Studio 2022 IDE (purple accents, standard VS menus, Solution Explorer), not Visual Studio Code or a basic text editor.

integrity.description: The project genuinely loaded as a structured solution.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Solution Explorer should show a proper C# project tree (e.g., Solution 'HelloWorld', Project 'HelloWorld', Dependencies, Program.cs), not just an isolated folder or file.
